playbook.intent: Alert fires → on-call triages within minutes → every alert closes with a verdict (noise / known / new incident) + audit log.
playbook.scope: An alert lands in production during business hours. On-call engineer triages within minutes using AI-classified context, decides: noise / known / new incident. Noise → mute with documented reason. Known → cross-link to existing thread + delta. New incident → enter the incident-postmortem-preventive-backlog playbook. Every alert closes with a written verdict + audit log entry.

success-criteria.criterion: Every alert reaches a verdict within target SLA
Audit log entry per alert with verdict + reasoning
Noise alerts have a mute justification logged
Known issues cross-linked to canonical thread
New incidents handed off to postmortem flow

stage.name: Receive + classify
stage.intent: AI classifier routes; on-call reads service-ownership map.

tasks.task: Read AI triage classification + suggested category
Check api-monitoring metrics + logs for context
Decide entry path: noise / known / new

stage.decision-gate: Advance only with an explicit verdict; "we'll see what it does" is not a verdict.

stage.name: Investigate under read-only default
stage.intent: If 'new', investigate read-only; respect approval-gate boundaries.

tasks.task: Walk runbook tagged steps for the service
Apply tool-tier approval gate for any write

stage.decision-gate: If escalation is needed, hand off to incident-postmortem-preventive-backlog cleanly. Don't keep investigating solo past 15 minutes.

stage.name: Close with verdict
stage.intent: Document verdict; trigger postmortem flow if needed.

tasks.task: Write verdict + reasoning to audit log
For known → cross-link to canonical thread

outputs.output: Linked artefacts (mute, thread, or postmortem draft)

stage.decision-gate: Required: written verdict. An alert that closes silently is a future trap.

extra.value: [Out-of-hours paging escalation — runbook handles that, Full postmortem flow — see incident-postmortem-preventive-backlog, Alert engineering / reducing noise upstream — separate workstream]
[Sentry / Datadog alerts wired with service-ownership map, AI triage classifier in front of the alert stream, Runbook system (markdown tagged steps)]

[Muting a 'noise' alert without documenting why — next on-call re-investigates from scratch, Skipping read-only default — accidental writes during investigation, Stretching investigation past 15 min instead of escalating, Closing alerts in chat-only without an audit log]
['Can I point to every alert from yesterday and read its verdict?', 'Did any noise mute carry an explicit reason and expiry?', 'Did the new incidents hand off cleanly to the postmortem flow?']

{human: faion get-content sentry-datadog-alert-triage --format md, agent: faion get-content sentry-datadog-alert-triage --format context, json: faion get-content sentry-datadog-alert-triage --format json}
[{version: 0.1.0, date: 2026-05-20, note: Initial draft for F-064 P6 product-dev team persona}]
